texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Abstract
texts.text: Abstract

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Seawater reverse osmosis desalination (SWRO) requires less energy compared with the distillation method and thus is an important technology except Middle Eastern countries where energy costs are higher. Recently, even Middle Eastern countries where the distillation method is still a major technology, have begun adopting the RO method in new desalination plants in line with government policy and the trend is for the development of larger (in excess of half mega-ton per day or mega-ton per day size) so-called 'Mega-SWRO' plants. With these trends in the global market, the requirements of sustainable SWRO desalination as green desalination for the 21 st century are summarized under three subjects: 1) Energy resources:
texts.text: Seawater reverse osmosis desalination (SWRO) requires less energy compared with the distillation method and thus is an important technology except Middle Eastern countries where energy costs are higher. Recently, even Middle Eastern countries where the distillation method is still a major technology, have begun adopting the RO method in new desalination plants in line with government policy and the trend is for the development of larger (in excess of half mega-ton per day or mega-ton per day size) so-called 'Mega-SWRO' plants. With these trends in the global market, the requirements of sustainable SWRO desalination as green desalination for the 21 st century are summarized under three subjects: 1) Energy resources:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Renewable energy, 2) Seawater RO system: Advanced membrane and membrane system, 3) Reduction of marine pollution: Green desalination. The 'Mega-ton Water System' project has been conducted to solve issues related to subjects 2) and 3) as Japanese national project.
texts.text: Renewable energy, 2) Seawater RO system: Advanced membrane and membrane system, 3) Reduction of marine pollution: Green desalination. The 'Mega-ton Water System' project has been conducted to solve issues related to subjects 2) and 3) as Japanese national project.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a. By combining a low pressure SWRO membrane and a low pressure two-stage high recovery SWRO system, 20% energy reduction was possible. And 30% energy saving in total was also possible as the SWRO-PRO hybrid system.
texts.text: By combining a low pressure SWRO membrane and a low pressure two-stage high recovery SWRO system, 20% energy reduction was possible. And 30% energy saving in total was also possible as the SWRO-PRO hybrid system.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b. For low environmental impact as green desalination, less chemical and less chemical cleaning for reliable operation have been established.
texts.text: For low environmental impact as green desalination, less chemical and less chemical cleaning for reliable operation have been established.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: c. Low-cost renewable energy, particularly solar energy, is now available to solve issues related to subject 1. By combining these sophisticated technologies, the cost of seawater desalination will be $ 0.50/ m 3 /day or less.
texts.text: Low-cost renewable energy, particularly solar energy, is now available to solve issues related to subject 1. By combining these sophisticated technologies, the cost of seawater desalination will be $ 0.50/ m 3 /day or less.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 1. Introduction
texts.text: 1. Introduction

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The United States began research and development of seawater desalination systems such as the distillation process and membrane process in the 1960s. The  distillation  process  became  major  technology  used  in  actual  plants  in the  1970s.  Since  2000,  transition  of  technology  from  the  distillation  to  the reverse osmosis membrane process has occurred as shown in Figure 1. Now, the  membrane  process  become  major  technology  (Desal  Data  2014/GWI/ Desalination Inventory) [1,2].
texts.text: The United States began research and development of seawater desalination systems such as the distillation process and membrane process in the 1960s. The  distillation  process  became  major  technology  used  in  actual  plants  in the  1970s.  Since  2000,  transition  of  technology  from  the  distillation  to  the reverse osmosis membrane process has occurred as shown in Figure 1. Now, the  membrane  process  become  major  technology  (Desal  Data  2014/GWI/ Desalination Inventory) [1,2].

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The scale of each desalination plants year by  year has been increasing, thus we predicted in 2009 that 'Mega-SWRO': large plants of the mega-ton per  day  scale  (1,000,000  m 3 /day)  would  be  required  from  market  by  2020. This prediction has been really realized now as shown on the right of Figure 4 [6].  Many  large  plants  over  the  500,000  m 3 /day,  the  so-called  'MegaSWRO,' started to construct them in 2018-2019 in Middle Eastern countries such as Saudi Arabia and the UAE.
texts.text: The scale of each desalination plants year by  year has been increasing, thus we predicted in 2009 that 'Mega-SWRO': large plants of the mega-ton per  day  scale  (1,000,000  m 3 /day)  would  be  required  from  market  by  2020. texts.label: text
texts.orig: Requirement for sustainable seawater desalination as green desalination for  the  21 st century  must  tackle  issues  related  to:  (1)  Energy  resources,  (2) Seawater RO system, and (3) Reduction of marine pollution. The 'Mega-ton Water System' project was conducted with the aim of developing sustainable water treatment core technologies. The missions of the seawater RO system were:  (1)  energy  saving  (20%  or  30%),  (2)  low  environmental  impact,  (3) reliable plant operation, (4) low water production cost as illustrated in Figure 6 [6,8,9].
texts.text: Requirement for sustainable seawater desalination as green desalination for  the  21 st century  must  tackle  issues  related  to:  (1)  Energy  resources,  (2) Seawater RO system, and (3) Reduction of marine pollution. The 'Mega-ton Water System' project was conducted with the aim of developing sustainable water treatment core technologies. The missions of the seawater RO system were:  (1)  energy  saving  (20%  or  30%),  (2)  low  environmental  impact,  (3) reliable plant operation, (4) low water production cost as illustrated in Figure 6 [6,8,9].

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The 'Mega-ton Water System' project envisions green desalination.
texts.text: The 'Mega-ton Water System' project envisions green desalination.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2. Results and discussion
texts.text: 2. Results and discussion

texts.label: text
texts.orig: As described in 1. Introduction, the global market for SWRO, especially in Middle Eastern countries, has changed with increases in plant size ('MegaSWRO') and decreases of specific energy consumption of the plant systems (SEC: kwh/ ㎥ ), followed by a reduction of the price of desalinated water.
texts.text: As described in 1. Introduction, the global market for SWRO, especially in Middle Eastern countries, has changed with increases in plant size ('MegaSWRO') and decreases of specific energy consumption of the plant systems (SEC: kwh/ ㎥ ), followed by a reduction of the price of desalinated water.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This  paradigm  shift  has  occurred  in  the  design  and  optimization  of SWRO plants [6].
texts.text: This  paradigm  shift  has  occurred  in  the  design  and  optimization  of SWRO plants [6].

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Another trend has been the continuous technological innovations such as the 'Mega-ton Water System' considering of energy saving, low environmental  impact  and  reliable  plant  operation  for  green  desalination [6,8,9].
texts.text: Another trend has been the continuous technological innovations such as the 'Mega-ton Water System' considering of energy saving, low environmental  impact  and  reliable  plant  operation  for  green  desalination [6,8,9].

texts.orig: The  subsequent  progress  of  the  'Mega-ton  Water  System'  is  shown  in Figure 7 [6]. A national research project was conducted from 2010 to 2013 and pilot plant (500 ㎥ /d)verification was conducted from 2016 to 2018 in Al Jubail,  Saudi  Arabia  to  confirm  the  following  three  advanced  technologies: ① The new advanced low pressure SWRO membrane ② low pressure twostage,  high  recovery SWRO system and ③ biofouling monitoring 'mBFR' technology.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Al-Jubail, Saudi Arabia in the Gulf is considered to be the most heavy biofouling area due to high salinity (4.6%), high organics and large temperature variations (14 to 37 ºC). Stable operation and expected operation cost  were  verified  over  one  year  of  operation,  by  complete  no-chlorine  and no-SBS dosing system [6]. The details were reported by Dr. Kuihara under the  title  'Further  Progress  of  'Mega-ton  Water  System'  Technology  for Green Desalination' at the Saudi Water Forum, 18 March 2019 and also Dr. Kurihara and Dr. Kurokawa under the title 'Further Progress of 'Mega-ton Water System' at the 12 th  SWA-APDA Joint Conference, Singapore 11 July 2018.
texts.text: Al-Jubail, Saudi Arabia in the Gulf is considered to be the most heavy biofouling area due to high salinity (4.6%), high organics and large temperature variations (14 to 37 ºC). Stable operation and expected operation cost  were  verified  over  one  year  of  operation,  by  complete  no-chlorine  and no-SBS dosing system [6]. The details were reported by Dr. Kuihara under the  title  'Further  Progress  of  'Mega-ton  Water  System'  Technology  for Green Desalination' at the Saudi Water Forum, 18 March 2019 and also Dr. Kurihara and Dr. Kurokawa under the title 'Further Progress of 'Mega-ton Water System' at the 12 th  SWA-APDA Joint Conference, Singapore 11 July 2018.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2.3.1. SWRO-PRO hybrid process
texts.text: 2.3.1. SWRO-PRO hybrid process

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The target of this research is 1) Energy recovery from concentrated brine by PRO system and 2) Reduce the concentration of brine discharged into the sea, in order to reduce environment impact. Thus Pressure Retarded Osmosis (PRO) technology (system) is to be added to the seawater desalination system as shown in Figure 11 [2,8,9].
texts.text: The target of this research is 1) Energy recovery from concentrated brine by PRO system and 2) Reduce the concentration of brine discharged into the sea, in order to reduce environment impact. Thus Pressure Retarded Osmosis (PRO) technology (system) is to be added to the seawater desalination system as shown in Figure 11 [2,8,9].

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The  installation  of  a  SWRO-PRO  hybrid  system  reduces  electricity consumption  per  unit  necessary  to  produce  water  by  10%  based  on  the calculation results of the demonstration plant.
texts.text: The  installation  of  a  SWRO-PRO  hybrid  system  reduces  electricity consumption  per  unit  necessary  to  produce  water  by  10%  based  on  the calculation results of the demonstration plant.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: 30% energy savings in total, including energy savings of 20%, SWRO of the 'Mega-ton Water System' are possible (Figure 12) [2,8,9].
texts.text: 30% energy savings in total, including energy savings of 20%, SWRO of the 'Mega-ton Water System' are possible (Figure 12) [2,8,9].

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2.3.2. Green desalination
texts.text: 2.3.2. Green desalination

texts.label: text
texts.orig: 10 years ago, there has been greater awareness of the need to preserve the environment, and there have been efforts to reduce the amount of chemically treated seawater discharged from desalination plants: the distillation and the membrane process, to lessen the ecological impact [10].
texts.text: 10 years ago, there has been greater awareness of the need to preserve the environment, and there have been efforts to reduce the amount of chemically treated seawater discharged from desalination plants: the distillation and the membrane process, to lessen the ecological impact [10].

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Background of 'Green Desalination' is based on the study 'The state of desalination  and  brine  production:  A  global  outlook  which  was  recently published  in  the  journal  'Science  of  the  Total  Environment':  Review:  the state  of  desalination  and  brine  production,  and  had  a  great  impact  on international media. The study was written by scientists from the Institute for Water,  the  Environment  and  Health  from  the  United  Nations  University (UNU-INWEH), Wageningen University (Holland), and the Gwangju Institute of Science and Technology (South Korea) [11].
texts.text: Background of 'Green Desalination' is based on the study 'The state of desalination  and  brine  production:  A  global  outlook  which  was  recently published  in  the  journal  'Science  of  the  Total  Environment':  Review:  the state  of  desalination  and  brine  production,  and  had  a  great  impact  on international media. The study was written by scientists from the Institute for Water,  the  Environment  and  Health  from  the  United  Nations  University (UNU-INWEH), Wageningen University (Holland), and the Gwangju Institute of Science and Technology (South Korea) [11].

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2.2.1. New advanced low pressure SWRO membrane
texts.text: 2.2.1. New advanced low pressure SWRO membrane

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The  history  of  the  high-performance  SWRO  membrane  and  the  new advanced low pressure SWRO membrane developed by Toray are shown in Figure 9  [6].  Since  the  1980s,  membrane  performance  of  cross-linked  fully aromatic  polyamide  composite  membrane  has  been  continuously  improved both in salt rejection (%) and permeate quantity ( ㎥ /d as 8-inch element) as shown on the left side of Figure 9 [6]. The right side of Figure 9 [6] shows a comparison  at  the  same  flux  base  between  the  latest  new  advanced  low pressure  SWRO  membrane  (2018)  and  the  2010s  membrane.  About  15% decrease  of  operating  pressure  was  confirmed  as  an  advantage  of  the  low pressure SWRO membrane.
texts.text: The  history  of  the  high-performance  SWRO  membrane  and  the  new advanced low pressure SWRO membrane developed by Toray are shown in Figure 9  [6].  Since  the  1980s,  membrane  performance  of  cross-linked  fully aromatic  polyamide  composite  membrane  has  been  continuously  improved both in salt rejection (%) and permeate quantity ( ㎥ /d as 8-inch element) as shown on the left side of Figure 9 [6]. The right side of Figure 9 [6] shows a comparison  at  the  same  flux  base  between  the  latest  new  advanced  low pressure  SWRO  membrane  (2018)  and  the  2010s  membrane.  About  15% decrease  of  operating  pressure  was  confirmed  as  an  advantage  of  the  low pressure SWRO membrane.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2.2.2. Low pressure two-stage high recovery SWRO system
texts.text: 2.2.2. Low pressure two-stage high recovery SWRO system

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The experimental results of the high recovery SWRO system are shown in Figure 10 [6].
texts.text: The experimental results of the high recovery SWRO system are shown in Figure 10 [6].

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1) Flux equalization & high recovery
texts.text: Flux equalization & high recovery

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: -  Almost  the  same  flux  distribution  as  that  of  the  conventional  system  was adjusted by applying back pressure to the first stage
texts.text: Almost  the  same  flux  distribution  as  that  of  the  conventional  system  was adjusted by applying back pressure to the first stage

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2) Energy recovery
texts.text: Energy recovery

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: - The energy of permeate back pressure recovered by ERD and about 12% of the pump head could be reduced
texts.text: The energy of permeate back pressure recovered by ERD and about 12% of the pump head could be reduced

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 3) Other feature: Lower membrane fouling
texts.text: Other feature: Lower membrane fouling

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: -  Lower  flow  rate  and  flux  at  the  first  stage  could  reduce  fouling  potential significantly
texts.text: Lower  flow  rate  and  flux  at  the  first  stage  could  reduce  fouling  potential significantly

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The  contents  of  the  study  by  the  United  Nations  University  were announced through two international TV media. The first one was Bloomberg News/ 9 January - 'Saudi thirst for water is creating a toxic brine problem'. The second one was BBC News/ 14 January 2019 - 'Concerns over increase in toxic brine from desalination plants'. It is unknown why both TV programs used the expression 'toxic brine,' because the above study did not mention toxic brine.
texts.text: The  contents  of  the  study  by  the  United  Nations  University  were announced through two international TV media. The first one was Bloomberg News/ 9 January - 'Saudi thirst for water is creating a toxic brine problem'. The second one was BBC News/ 14 January 2019 - 'Concerns over increase in toxic brine from desalination plants'. It is unknown why both TV programs used the expression 'toxic brine,' because the above study did not mention toxic brine.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Saudi  Water  Conversion  Corporation  (SWCC)  responded  sensitively  to the  above  journal  &  TV  News,  using  advanced  technology  and  science, emphasizing that water environment management is carried out at all stages of project development and implementation from planning to design, construction and operation. Furthermore, the Desalination Technology Research  Institute  of  SWCC  (DTRI)  has  announced  that  it  is  promoting  a green desalination initiative, which has already produced tangible results [13].
texts.text: Saudi  Water  Conversion  Corporation  (SWCC)  responded  sensitively  to the  above  journal  &  TV  News,  using  advanced  technology  and  science, emphasizing that water environment management is carried out at all stages of project development and implementation from planning to design, construction and operation. Furthermore, the Desalination Technology Research  Institute  of  SWCC  (DTRI)  has  announced  that  it  is  promoting  a green desalination initiative, which has already produced tangible results [13].

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The International Desalination Association (IDA) also responded to this journal and TV News, and in an effort to publicize the IDA's response to the IDA Global Connections, conducted an opinion gathering survey on energy and  environment  experts  regarding  the  environmental  impact  of  seawater
texts.text: The International Desalination Association (IDA) also responded to this journal and TV News, and in an effort to publicize the IDA's response to the IDA Global Connections, conducted an opinion gathering survey on energy and  environment  experts  regarding  the  environmental  impact  of  seawater

texts.orig: Study highlighted
texts.text: Study highlighted

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: ･ The  need  of  unconventional  water  resources  as  key  to  supporting  SDG  6 achievement.
texts.text: ･ The  need  of  unconventional  water  resources  as  key  to  supporting  SDG  6 achievement.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: ･ Water production by desalination is 95.37 million ㎥ /day.
texts.text: ･ Water production by desalination is 95.37 million ㎥ /day.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: ･ Brine  production  and  energy  consumption  are  key  barriers  to  desalination expansion [11, 12].
texts.text: ･ Brine  production  and  energy  consumption  are  key  barriers  to  desalination expansion [11, 12].

texts.label: text
texts.orig: About  half  of  the  world's  desalinated  seawater  is  produced  in  four countries  in  the  Middle  East.  In  order  to  reduce  negative  impacts  on  the environment  and  reduce  the  cost  of  water  production,  we  have  proposed innovative  financial  mechanisms  and  technological  improvements  (such  as the development of commercial recovery and production systems for salts and metals). These solutions need to be affordable so they can be extended to low and middle income countries.
texts.text: About  half  of  the  world's  desalinated  seawater  is  produced  in  four countries  in  the  Middle  East.  In  order  to  reduce  negative  impacts  on  the environment  and  reduce  the  cost  of  water  production,  we  have  proposed innovative  financial  mechanisms  and  technological  improvements  (such  as the development of commercial recovery and production systems for salts and metals). These solutions need to be affordable so they can be extended to low and middle income countries.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The purpose of this research is energy saving, cost saving and environmental impact  reduction,  which  is  the  same  direction  as  the  'Mega-ton  Water System' project as shown in Figure 6 [6, 8, 9].
texts.text: The purpose of this research is energy saving, cost saving and environmental impact  reduction,  which  is  the  same  direction  as  the  'Mega-ton  Water System' project as shown in Figure 6 [6, 8, 9].

texts.label: text
texts.orig: History of anti-biofouling trials and new system for future SWRO system:
texts.text: History of anti-biofouling trials and new system for future SWRO system:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: As  shown  in  Figure  13  [6,8,9],  anti-biofouling  trials  for  the  SWRO system were conducted as follows.
texts.text: As  shown  in  Figure  13  [6,8,9],  anti-biofouling  trials  for  the  SWRO system were conducted as follows.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1) Initial stage: Conventional SWRO system since 1990.
texts.text: Initial stage: Conventional SWRO system since 1990.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This  system  is  still  used  in  special  cases  such  as  when  there  is cooperation with the power plants.
texts.text: This  system  is  still  used  in  special  cases  such  as  when  there  is cooperation with the power plants.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In  this  system,  sterilization  by  chlorination  was  considered  common sense, but frequent biofouling occurred and frequent chemical cleaning was  performed.  For  example,  two  weeks  of  operation  required  one week of cleaning.
texts.text: In  this  system,  sterilization  by  chlorination  was  considered  common sense, but frequent biofouling occurred and frequent chemical cleaning was  performed.  For  example,  two  weeks  of  operation  required  one week of cleaning.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2) Intermediate stage: current SWRO system (2004~)
texts.text: Intermediate stage: current SWRO system (2004~)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Many  global  specialists  on  SWRO  plant  operation  came  together  in Florida  &  San  Diego  in  2004,  and  discussed  the  matter  of  heavy biofouling of SWRO plants.
texts.text: Many  global  specialists  on  SWRO  plant  operation  came  together  in Florida  &  San  Diego  in  2004,  and  discussed  the  matter  of  heavy biofouling of SWRO plants.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: As  a solution, mild  sterilization  from  continuous  chlorination  to intermittent  chlorination  has  been  adopted.  However,  biofouling  has not  been  eliminated,  and  there  are  doubts  concerning  the  efficacy  of chlorine sterilization.
texts.text: As  a solution, mild  sterilization  from  continuous  chlorination  to intermittent  chlorination  has  been  adopted.  However,  biofouling  has not  been  eliminated,  and  there  are  doubts  concerning  the  efficacy  of chlorine sterilization.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 3) Ideal  stage:  Future  SWRO  system  (2017~)  presented  by  'Mega-ton Water System' project
texts.text: Ideal  stage:  Future  SWRO  system  (2017~)  presented  by  'Mega-ton Water System' project

texts.label: text
texts.orig: As the ideal stage, future SWRO system (2017~) has been presented by the 'Mega-ton Water System' project based on the data [8,9].
texts.text: As the ideal stage, future SWRO system (2017~) has been presented by the 'Mega-ton Water System' project based on the data [8,9].

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This is a paradigm shift in understanding that chlorine sterilization and SBS dosing triggers biofouling. Thus, the new system has no chlorination and no de-chlorination.  As a result, biofouling can be controlled even in highly saline  seawater such  as that of  the Gulf Sea. Recommendation: this method optimized the pretreatment conditions by monitoring the 'mBFR': membrane biofilm formation rate.
texts.text: This is a paradigm shift in understanding that chlorine sterilization and SBS dosing triggers biofouling. Thus, the new system has no chlorination and no de-chlorination.  As a result, biofouling can be controlled even in highly saline  seawater such  as that of  the Gulf Sea. Recommendation: this method optimized the pretreatment conditions by monitoring the 'mBFR': membrane biofilm formation rate.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2.3.4. Chlorine sterilization has no effect on marine bacteria.
texts.text: 2.3.4. Chlorine sterilization has no effect on marine bacteria.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: As  shown  in  Figure  14,  the  effect  of  chlorine  sterilization  on  marine bacteria  was  compared  evaluated  by  the  conventional  method  (Plate  count method) and by fluorescence microscopic observation.
texts.text: As  shown  in  Figure  14,  the  effect  of  chlorine  sterilization  on  marine bacteria  was  compared  evaluated  by  the  conventional  method  (Plate  count method) and by fluorescence microscopic observation.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: As already reported by the authors as shown in Figure 14 [15], the plate count method can be overestimated and Fluorescence microscopic observation shows that much higher numbers of marine bacteria survive after chlorination than expected.
texts.text: As already reported by the authors as shown in Figure 14 [15], the plate count method can be overestimated and Fluorescence microscopic observation shows that much higher numbers of marine bacteria survive after chlorination than expected.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Chlorine sterilization of seawater is not only ineffective, it is counterproductive.
texts.text: Chlorine sterilization of seawater is not only ineffective, it is counterproductive.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2.3.7. Chlorine sterilization and SBS dosing triggers biofouling
texts.text: 2.3.7. Chlorine sterilization and SBS dosing triggers biofouling

texts.label: text
texts.orig: In real SWRO system as shown in Figure 13, Initial stage: conventional SWRO system - continuous chlorination and continuous de-chlorination and Ideal  stage:  Future  SWRO  system  -no  chlorination  and  no  de-chlorination were compared in pilot plant as shown in Figure 16 [6]. From this figure, no chlorination,  no  de-chlorination  as  Future  SWRO  system,  show  that  no biofouling  compared  to  the  heavy  biofouling  of  continuous  chlorination, continues  de-chlorination  as  Initial  stage.  Chlorine  sterilization  and  SBS dosing triggers heavy biofouling.
texts.text: In real SWRO system as shown in Figure 13, Initial stage: conventional SWRO system - continuous chlorination and continuous de-chlorination and Ideal  stage:  Future  SWRO  system  -no  chlorination  and  no  de-chlorination were compared in pilot plant as shown in Figure 16 [6]. From this figure, no chlorination,  no  de-chlorination  as  Future  SWRO  system,  show  that  no biofouling  compared  to  the  heavy  biofouling  of  continuous  chlorination, continues  de-chlorination  as  Initial  stage.  Chlorine  sterilization  and  SBS dosing triggers heavy biofouling.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2.3.5. 'mBFR': Membrane biofilm formation rate
texts.text: 2.3.5. 'mBFR': Membrane biofilm formation rate

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The  original  'mBFR'  measuring  protocol  was  reported  by  the  authors [15], after that, 'mBFR' measurement were much improved the accuracy and reliability of the biofilm formation evaluation as shown in Figure 15 by Ito et al. [6].
texts.text: The  original  'mBFR'  measuring  protocol  was  reported  by  the  authors [15], after that, 'mBFR' measurement were much improved the accuracy and reliability of the biofilm formation evaluation as shown in Figure 15 by Ito et al. [6].

texts.label: text
texts.orig: 'mBFR' was developed by  Toray as an indicator  of  the  seawater  RO feed water.
texts.text: 'mBFR' was developed by  Toray as an indicator  of  the  seawater  RO feed water.

texts.label: page_header

texts.text: M. Kurihara and Y. Ito / Journal of Membrane Science and Research 6 (2020) 20-29

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2.3.8. Quantitative RO chemical cleaning interval due to biofouling
texts.text: 2.3.8. Quantitative RO chemical cleaning interval due to biofouling

texts.label: text
texts.orig: As shown on the left side of Figure 17, the points of 'mBFR' are very important, especially the value of 'mBFR' monitoring 2 [6] . The right side of  Figure  17  shows  the  relationship  between  'mBFR'  and  the  chemical cleaning interval. If the 'mBFR' value at monitoring 2 is less than 10, the chemical  cleaning  interval  will  be  once  or  twice  a  year. Low  'mBFR' is necessary for reliable operation of a SWRO plant with less chemical cleaning. The  chemical  cleaning  interval  can  be  predicted  with  'mBFR'  of  RO  feed water [6]. The details were presented by Mr. Y. Ito, S. Kitade and Y. Tanaka under the title 'Clarification of Impact of Biofouling Triggered by Chemical Addition'  at  IDA  World  Congress,  Tianjin,  China  2013  and  Mr.  Y  Ito,  S. Kantani,  T.  Maeda,  K.Okubo  and  M.  Taniguchi under  the  title  'Innovative Biofouling  Monitoring  Device  and  its  Criteria  for  Reverse  Osmosis  plant Operation and Optimization' at IDA World Congress, San Diego, CA, U.S.A
texts.text: As shown on the left side of Figure 17, the points of 'mBFR' are very important, especially the value of 'mBFR' monitoring 2 [6] . The right side of  Figure  17  shows  the  relationship  between  'mBFR'  and  the  chemical cleaning interval. If the 'mBFR' value at monitoring 2 is less than 10, the chemical  cleaning  interval  will  be  once  or  twice  a  year. Low  'mBFR' is necessary for reliable operation of a SWRO plant with less chemical cleaning. The  chemical  cleaning  interval  can  be  predicted  with  'mBFR'  of  RO  feed water [6]. The details were presented by Mr. Y. Ito, S. Kitade and Y. Tanaka under the title 'Clarification of Impact of Biofouling Triggered by Chemical Addition'  at  IDA  World  Congress,  Tianjin,  China  2013  and  Mr.  Y  Ito,  S. Kantani,  T.  Maeda,  K.Okubo  and  M.  Taniguchi under  the  title  'Innovative Biofouling  Monitoring  Device  and  its  Criteria  for  Reverse  Osmosis  plant Operation and Optimization' at IDA World Congress, San Diego, CA, U.S.A

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 3. Conclusions
texts.text: 3. Conclusions

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1) SWRO has become major technology even in Middle Eastern countries and new large plants in excess of half mega-ton per day or mega-ton per day, so- called 'Mega-SWRO' are expected to be contracted over the next 12 months.
texts.text: SWRO has become major technology even in Middle Eastern countries and new large plants in excess of half mega-ton per day or mega-ton per day, so- called 'Mega-SWRO' are expected to be contracted over the next 12 months.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 2) Requirements for sustainable SWRO desalination as green desalination for 21 st century are four key challenges as follows,
texts.text: Requirements for sustainable SWRO desalination as green desalination for 21 st century are four key challenges as follows,

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: (1)Energy  saving,  (2)  Low  environment  Impact,  (3)  Reliable  Plant operation, (4) Low water production cost.
texts.text: (1)Energy  saving,  (2)  Low  environment  Impact,  (3)  Reliable  Plant operation, (4) Low water production cost.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 3)  Solar  power  energy  became  promising candidate  for  energy  resources  as renewable energy.
texts.text: Solar  power  energy  became  promising candidate  for  energy  resources  as renewable energy.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 4) Progress of innovative new advanced low pressure SWRO membrane and two-stage  high  recovery  SWRO  system  are  contributed  to  the  20% energy saving and 30% energy saving was confirmed by the SWRO-PRO hybrid system of the 'Mega-ton Water System' project.
texts.text: Progress of innovative new advanced low pressure SWRO membrane and two-stage  high  recovery  SWRO  system  are  contributed  to  the  20% energy saving and 30% energy saving was confirmed by the SWRO-PRO hybrid system of the 'Mega-ton Water System' project.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 5)  Future  SWRO system as reliable  plant  operation  was  proposed by  using no-chlorination  and  no-de  chlorination  system  with  mBFR  monitoring technology.
texts.text: Future  SWRO system as reliable  plant  operation  was  proposed by  using no-chlorination  and  no-de  chlorination  system  with  mBFR  monitoring technology.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 6) Verification of this system was done in a pilot, located in Al Jubail over one  year,  and  full  scale  plant  verification  will  be  conducted  in  Umluji, Saudi Arabia.
texts.text: Verification of this system was done in a pilot, located in Al Jubail over one  year,  and  full  scale  plant  verification  will  be  conducted  in  Umluji, Saudi Arabia.
